name: Linked Facts
description: Facts with explicit relationships between concepts, designed to provoke typed link creation across the full ontology: causes, caused_by, depends_on, requires, contains, part_of, supports, contradicts, precedes, follows.
statements: My peanut allergy caused me to always carry an EpiPen whenever I go out.
The cold, snowy winters in Aosta are what caused me to take up skiing.
My frustration with monolithic architectures early in my career caused me to specialise in distributed systems.
My interest in actor-based concurrency was caused by repeated failures I experienced with shared-state threading models.
The Rorschach framework depends on the Bun runtime for its execution environment and module system.
Mountain biking in Aosta depends on good trail conditions, which are only available from May to October.
Jazz piano improvisation requires a solid foundation in music theory and knowledge of scales and chord voicings.
Running the Rorschach development setup requires macOS because of specific native tooling I rely on.
The Rorschach framework contains three main subsystems: an actor model runtime, a plugin system, and a persistent memory module.
The memory module is part of the Rorschach framework and handles long-term knowledge storage.
The actor model runtime is part of the Rorschach framework and manages message passing between actors.
My positive experience building Rorschach entirely in TypeScript supports my preference for static typing in large projects.
My enjoyment of spontaneous jazz improvisation somewhat contradicts my preference for strict type systems and formal structure in programming.
I finished reading Dune on March 12 2026, which preceded my trip to the Lisbon tech conference in April 2026.
I bought my Keychron K2 keyboard on February 1 2026, which preceded the start of the Rorschach project.
My evening herbal tea routine follows my morning double espresso as part of a deliberate caffeine tapering strategy.
My specialisation in distributed systems follows from over a decade of working on large-scale backend infrastructure.
